free_text: Excitation functions and production rates of relevance
to the production of In-111 by proton bombardment of
Cd-nat and In-nat up to 100 MeV.
Intrinsic Ge detector with a relative
efficiency of 13% and a resolution of 1.8 keV FWHM
at 1.33 MeV.
.The computer code 'ALICE'.
The total uncertainties in the cross-sections
and production rates were obtained by summing all the
contributing uncertainties in quadrature. They are
expressed with a 68% confidence level,and consist of:
* a variable component, due to counting statistics and
photopeak integration;
The error in beam-loss corrections;
Comprising uncertainties in beam current;
The error in the detector efficiency;
The error in counting geometry;
The error in decay corrections;
The error in foil thickness;
The uncertainties in incident energies.
SD:Updated to new date formats,lower case.
SF4 corrected in REACTION code in Subent 002 according
to author's remark. DATA-ERR -> ERR-T in Subents 02,03.
Info from COMMENT moved to ERR-ANALYS.ERR-ANALYS update
MONITOR added. DECAY-DATA were added to Subent 002.
SD: Major revision in 003.
SD: Corrections in all Subents. Subents
were split.
Natural high-purity Cd foils (99.95%), 125 mu-m thick.
Each target foil was followed by a 250 mu-m high-purity
Al monitor foil (99.999%) for the accurate measurement
of the incident proton flux.
Tbl. 1
SD: SF4=In-110m -> In-110g in REACTION (2)
code according to author's remark.
SD: STATUS updated. Data correction at
E=65.7 MeV (98.7 -> 98.6 mb). Subent was split in four.
Each 125 mu-m thick high-purity In foil(99.99%) was
sandwiched between two 20 mu-m Al microfoils for
excluding local melting and subsequent sticking of
the foils.
Tbl. 3
